creator: billf@chc-chimes.com
text:
On Sat, 24 Oct 1998 root@mc5sys.in-berlin.de wrote:

> >Number:         8432
> >Synopsis:       New Port: qtarch-1.4-1

> sed 's/^X//' >qtarch/Makefile << 'END-of-qtarch/Makefile'

> X# $Id: Makefile,v 1.0 1998/10/17 22:17:30 fmg Exp $

Why is there an $ID tag on a port that hasn't been committed yet?

> XDISTNAME=	qtarch-1.4-1

A 'PKGNAME=	qtarch-1.4.1' would be appropriate.

> XCATEGORIES=	devel x11

As Satoshi once commented on my port, "this probably doesn't belong in
x11, just because it is an X app".

> XEXTRACT_SUFX=	.tar.gz

This is redundant. .tar.gz is implied.
